markets.question: Sabres vs. Bruins: O/U 5.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for May 1 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Sabres and Bruins combine to score 6 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 6, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Eastern Conference first-round Stanley Cup Playoffs series, Buffalo Sabres hold a 3-2 lead over the Boston Bruins heading into Game 6 at KeyBank Center, following Boston's 2-1 overtime victory in Game 5 on April 28 where David Pastrnak scored the winner and Jeremy Swayman made 24 saves. Sabres goaltender Devon Levi has been stellar with key stops in recent wins, but depth forward Noah Ostlund exited Game 5 with a lower-body injury expected to sideline him, while Bruins winger Viktor Arvidsson remains out with an upper-body issue from Game 4's 6-1 loss. Buffalo's home-ice advantage and momentum from taking Games 3 and 4 contrast Boston's desperation and historical edge in head-to-head matchups (11-7-2 last 20 regular-season games), tightening trader consensus on a closely contested elimination clash amid injury impacts and strong goaltending duels.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's short-term forecast and late-day observations drive the near-unanimous trader consensus for a highest temperature of 25°C or higher on April 30, with current readings at 23.8°C by 19:50 HKT confirming daytime peaks met that threshold amid mainly cloudy skies, moderate easterly winds, and low rain probability. This aligns with HKO's outlook for 2026 as one of the city's hottest years on record, following mid-April highs exceeding 30°C and seasonal above-normal temperatures favoring late-spring warming. Model consensus and historical April averages around 26°C reinforce the positioning, though minimal uncertainty remains from potential station-specific anomalies or unforecasted evening showers capping the official max below 25°C—scenarios deemed improbable by market-implied odds. Resolution awaits HKO's final daily record.

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 200+ posts from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between April 28, 12:00 PM ET and May 5, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This keeps 60-79 (40.5%), 80-99 (40.5%), and 100-119 (40.0%) tightly clustered, as minor daily variances from Verdict podcast promotions, Senate commentary, and Houston Rockets playoff updates drive totals. Separation could arise from intensified floor votes, committee hearings, or breaking foreign policy developments like Strait of Hormuz tensions, potentially pushing volume toward 120+.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Open Market Committee held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% following its April 28-29, 2026 meeting, amid a surge in March CPI inflation to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since mid-2024—driven by energy price spikes from geopolitical tensions. This decision, marked by rare dissents and Chair Powell's confirmation of staying on as a governor post-term, underscores policymaker caution as labor markets remain resilient with unemployment at 4.4%. The March dot plot median projects gradual easing to 3.4% by end-2026 and low-3% levels by 2027, aligning with trader consensus on Polymarket where lower rate thresholds before 2027 command strong implied probabilities. Key catalysts include June 16-17 FOMC and upcoming April CPI data, with futures implying limited near-term cuts absent cooling inflation.

markets.question: Will the U.S. flu hospitalization rate per 100,000 in Week 16 be between 90 and 95?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the cumulative influenza-associated hospitalization rate per 100,000 population for the United States, as reported for the specified week.

If the recorded data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be CDC FluView / FluSurv-NET (see: https://www.cdc.gov/fluview/index.html). If the FluSurv-NET hospitalization rate for the specified week is not released by 11:59 PM ET on the tenth calendar day following the date of the prior FluView weekly report release, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

Note: Only the CDC FluSurv-NET cumulative hospitalization rate per 100,000 population for the specified week will qualify, regardless of estimates, projections, state-level reports, or other influenza surveillance metrics published by the CDC or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: CDC FluSurv-NET data from the Week 15 FluView report, released April 24, show a cumulative influenza-associated hospitalization rate of 85.2 per 100,000 population—the third highest at this point since 2010-11—driven by earlier seasonal peaks in influenza B activity, now trending downward with a weekly rate of just 0.5 per 100,000, down from 0.7 the prior week. This minimal weekly addition, amid declining national flu activity, underpins the market's strong 92.8% implied probability for 85–90, reflecting trader consensus on stabilization as the 2025-26 season wanes. Scenarios challenging this include unexpected reporting lags inflating prior weeks or a rare late surge in cases, though epidemiological trends and low test positivity make such shifts unlikely; watch for the Week 16 FluView update expected early May.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 60-79 posts from April 24 to May 1, 2026?
markets.description:

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Los Angeles be between 56-57°F on May 2?

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasts cap Lucknow's maximum temperature at 36°C on May 2 under mainly clear skies, with minimums around 24°C, anchoring trader consensus at 52.5% implied probability for 36°C or below and 26.5% for exactly 37°C. This follows a recent high of 39.3°C on April 29 amid pre-monsoon warmth, but models project a 2-3°C rise through May 2 across northwest India—including Uttar Pradesh—tempered by partly cloudy conditions and isolated thundershowers earlier in the week, limiting intensification. Urban heat island effects and surface measurement variability introduce uncertainty, with national guidance anticipating a post-May 2 cooldown. Traders await IMD's May 1 update for refined ensemble model runs.

markets.question: Hawks vs. Knicks
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 2 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Hawks win, the market will resolve to "Hawks".
If the Knicks win, the market will resolve to "Knicks".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Knicks hold a 64% implied probability as Game 5 favorites at Madison Square Garden in this tied 2-2 first-round NBA playoff series, driven by their pristine injury report contrasting Atlanta Hawks center Jock Landale's absence with a right ankle sprain that thins frontcourt depth behind Onyeka Okongwu. Knicks seized momentum in Game 4's 16-point rout, fueled by Karl-Anthony Towns' playoff triple-double (20-10-10) and defensive clamps limiting Hawks to 24% three-point shooting on 41 tries plus 18 turnovers, exposing Atlanta's half-court woes despite CJ McCollum and Jalen Johnson's efforts. Home-court edge, elite paint efficiency (1.19 PPP), and Jalen Brunson's recovery from a minor ankle tweak bolster trader consensus on New York's path to a 3-2 lead.
